data.title: Hagerty is the best performing insurance broker YTD
data.description:
The S&P 500 financials sector has largely kept pace with the broader market in 2025, rising about 14% year to date.

Financials rank as the fourth-best performing sector in the S&P 500 (SP500).

As we look at the sub-sectors within the broader financial sector, below is a list of insurance broker stocks ranked by their YTD performance. The list includes each company’s SA Quant rating.

The list is topped by Hagerty (HGTY), with a YTD performance of +40.52% and a Strong Buy Quant Rating of 4.82. Willis Towers Watson Public (WTW) comes in second with a 6.12% YTD gain, followed by Aon plc (AON) at -1.03%.

The remaining stocks on the list exhibit negative YTD performance. Arthur J. Gallagher (AJG), Marsh & McLennan Companies (MMC), and Ryan Specialty (RYAN) occupy the middle positions. The Baldwin Insurance Group (BWIN) shows the lowest performance at -38.36% with a Strong Sell Quant Rating of 1.39.

Here is the list:

  1. Hagerty (HGTY), YTD perf: +40.52%, Quant Rating: Strong Buy (4.82)

  2. Willis Towers Watson (WTW), YTD perf: +6.12%, Quant Rating: Hold (2.97)

  3. Aon plc (AON), YTD perf: -1.03%, Quant Rating: Hold (3.00)

  4. Arthur J. Gallagher (AJG), YTD perf: -9.02%, Quant Rating: Sell (1.97)

  5. Marsh & McLennan Companies (MMC), YTD perf: -12.66%, Quant Rating: Hold (2.96)

  6. Ryan Specialty Holdings (RYAN), YTD perf: -18.94%, Quant Rating: Sell (1.60)

  7. Brown & Brown (BRO), YTD perf: -21.22%, Quant Rating: Hold (3.03)

  8. Goosehead Insurance (GSHD), YTD perf: -30.05%, Quant Rating: Sell (1.83)

  9. The Baldwin Insurance Group (BWIN), YTD perf: -38.36%, Quant Rating: Strong Sell (1.39)

Financials ETFs: (XLF), (VFH), (IYF), (FNCL), (IYG), and (FXO)

data.title: Orix is the top diversified financial services stock in terms of YTD performance
data.description:
As 2025 draws to a close, Wall Street has observed another strong year of gains across most sectors. 

The financial sector has emerged as the fourth best-performing one YTD, with an advance of +14.73%. Its accompanying State Street Financial Select Sector SPDR ETF (XLF) is +14.69% YTD.

Seeking Alpha took a look at the financials subsector that covers companies providing diversified financial services and identified the top YTD performers. Orix (IX) grabbed the number one spot with a +36.4% gain. 

Here is the list:

  1. Orix (IX), Quant Rating: Hold, 2.86; +36.4% YTD.
  2. Jackson Financial (JXN), Quant Rating: Hold, 3.43; +23.6% YTD. 
  3. Voya Financial (VOYA), Quant Rating: Hold, 3.12; +10.2% YTD. 
  4. Equitable (EQH), Quant Rating: Sell, 2.27; +2.8% YTD. 
  5. Apollo (APO), Quant Rating: Hold, 3.34; -9.9% YTD. 

Here are some financial sector-focused exchange-traded funds: (XLF), (VFH), (IYF), (FNCL), (IYG), and (FXO).

data.title: U.S. moves closer to approving deep-sea mining in global waters
data.description:
The U.S. government is moving closer to approving deep-sea mining in international waters, opening a contentious new front in the push to secure critical minerals.

Federal officials said they will hold public hearings next month on applications submitted by Canada-based The Metals Company (TMC), marking the first time Washington has formally considered permits for seabed mining beyond U.S. jurisdiction. 

The move follows an executive order signed by President Donald Trump in April directing agencies to accelerate approvals for mining in both domestic and international waters.

The applications seek permission to explore areas of the Pacific Ocean rich in mineral-laden nodules containing nickel, cobalt, manganese and copper — materials used in batteries and other technologies the administration has framed as strategically important for national security.

The National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration posted the proposals for public comment and said it is working to move the review process forward without unnecessary delays. The company has said it aims to begin commercial mining by 2027, a timeline legal and environmental experts say could face significant hurdles.

Deep-sea mining has never been carried out commercially, and the U.S. process would effectively bypass the International Seabed Authority, a U.N.-affiliated body negotiating global mining rules. The United States has not ratified the Law of the Sea treaty underpinning that framework, creating legal uncertainty that could invite court challenges or diplomatic disputes.

Environmental opposition remains strong. Dozens of countries have called for a moratorium or ban on seabed mining, and scientists warn that little is known about the long-term effects on deep-ocean ecosystems. The company would be required to submit a full environmental-impact statement if the U.S. review proceeds.

Economic questions also loom. Some analysts say demand for seabed minerals may not rise as sharply as expected, citing shifts in battery technology and existing supply from land-based producers.

data.title: SeaStar Medical announces 1-for-10 reverse split
data.description:
• SeaStar Medical (ICU) on Tuesday said it will implement a 1-for-10 reverse stock split of its common shares, effective January 5, 2026.
  • The company’s shares will begin trading on a post-split basis on the Nasdaq Capital Market at the market open on January 5 under the same ticker symbol, ICU. The move was approved by shareholders on December 18.
  • SeaStar said the reverse split is intended to raise its share price to help regain compliance with Nasdaq’s $1.00 minimum bid price requirement.
  • Each 10 shares outstanding will be consolidated into one share, with fractional shares rounded up to the nearest whole share.
  • ICU -0.0% after hours to $0.2352.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Henry Schein expects to appoint next CEO by mid-January
data.description:
Henry Schein (HSIC) said it expects to appoint its next CEO by mid-January.“We are pleased with the progress we have made in our search for Henry Schein’s next CEO," Philip A. Laskawy, lead director for Henry Schein said in a statement on Tuesday. "The search process is nearing completion but will not be finalized before year-end. We have evaluated a pool of highly qualified candidates, and the level of interest and caliber of talent have been exceptional."

Henry Schein (HSIC) announced in July that its CEO Stanley Bergman would be retiring at the end of 2025.

Bergman will now remain in his role as chairman and CEO of Henry Schein (HSIC) until a new CEO is appointed.

data.title: Nanalysis upsizes private placement, closes first tranche
data.description:
• Nanalysis Scientific (NSCI:CA) said on Tuesday it has closed the first tranche of a previously announced non-brokered private placement, raising about $2.5 million in gross proceeds.
  • The company issued roughly 16.5 million units at $0.15 per unit and said the proceeds will be used for debt reduction. It expects to close a second tranche in January 2026.
  • Nanalysis also said it has increased the size of the offering due to strong investor demand, allowing for the issuance of up to an additional 23,333,333 units for total gross proceeds of up to $3.5 million, subject to approval by the TSX Venture Exchange.
  • Additional funds will be used for debt reduction and magnet inventory purchases.
  • NSCIF closed -0.0% at $0.115.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: ARMOUR Residential REIT reports Q4 results
data.description:
• ARMOUR Residential REIT press release (ARR): Q4 The Company ended the quarter with revenues, $210.2M
  • net income to common,$156.3M
  • EPS of $1.49.
  • ARR also saw impressive economic net interest income of $91.6M, which is an increase from the $65.4M posted last year.
  • Economic interest rate was 5.0% for the quarter and increased from 4.78% last quarter while economic interest expense was 3.17%, a smaller increase from 2.96% last quarter.
  • This led to an economic interest spread of 1.83%. Distributable earnings for the quarter were $75.3M or $0.72 per share.
  • Distributable EPS declined 7.0% sequentially and 28.3% year over year. Finally, the Company ended the quarter with a BV/Share of $17.49 which is an increase of 3.5% compared to $16.90 at the end of last quarter. This gain in BV, combined with common dividends, resulted in a total economic return of 7.75% for the quarter.
  •  Armour was once again a strong dividend payer in the quarter. Total dividend payments were $0.72 per common share in the quarter, which is a monthly rate of $0.24.

data.title: Ramaco announces $100 million stock repurchase plan
data.description:
• Ramaco Resources (METC) announced on Tuesday that the board of directors has authorized the initiation of a share repurchase program of up to $100 million of the currently outstanding shares of its Class A common stock over a period of 24 months.
  • The Board also authorized the company to enter into written trading plans under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, to facilitate the repurchase of its common stock pursuant to its share repurchase program.
  • "We're excited to introduce a share repurchase program as another means of returning capital to our shareholders, which we are introducing as a matter of good corporate governance," said Randall Atkins, Chairman and CEO of Ramaco. "With the closing of Ramaco's previously-announced $600 plus million (before fees) in capital raises in the second half of 2025, we are well-positioned to pursue opportunities to unlock value and maximize shareholder returns. The share repurchase program highlights our confidence in our continued operational performance as well as financial strength."
  • The company said it intends to purchase shares under the repurchase program opportunistically with available funds, while maintaining sufficient liquidity to fund its capital development program.
  • METC +3.86% after hours to $17.48.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Data Storage extends expiration time of offer to purchase
data.description:
• Data Storage (DTST) announced on Tuesday that it has extended the expiration time of its offer to purchase to January 12, 2026, from the previously scheduled January 7, 2026.
  • The Company will file Amendment No. 2 to its Schedule TO, extending the tender offer expiration to January 12, 2026, at 12:00 midnight (New York City time), unless extended or terminated earlier.

data.title: CASI Pharmaceuticals wins Nasdaq extension to meet listing rules
data.description:
• CASI Pharmaceuticals (CASI) said on Tuesday it has received an extension from a Nasdaq hearings panel to remain listed, giving the company until February 17, 2026 to regain compliance with the exchange’s minimum market value of listed securities requirement.
  • The exchange had previously notified the company that it was out of compliance with the $35 million threshold after its market value fell below the requirement for 30 consecutive trading days.
  • CASI was initially given until November 3, 2025 to regain compliance.
  • The panel granted the extension after reviewing steps taken by the company, including management changes, financing actions, a convertible note purchase agreement, additional funding initiatives, and plans to divest its China assets.
  • The extension stays any suspension of CASI’s securities while it works to meet the requirement.
  • CASI -1.73% after hours to $0.7587.
  • Source: Press Release
